query: lung squamous cell carcinoma CT imaging central hilar

articles.title: CT Imaging Characteristics of Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Lung: A Comprehensive Review
articles.abstract: Squamous cell carcinoma typically presents as a central hilar mass on CT imaging, often with bronchial obstruction and post-obstructive atelectasis or pneumonia. Cavitation is common, occurring in approximately 30% of cases. The tumor frequently shows endobronchial growth pattern with irregular bronchial wall thickening and luminal narrowing.

articles.title: Differentiation of Central Lung Squamous Cell Carcinoma from Small Cell Carcinoma on CT
articles.abstract: Central squamous cell carcinoma demonstrates distinct CT features compared to small cell carcinoma. Squamous cell tumors show more irregular borders, frequent cavitation, and direct bronchial invasion. Post-contrast enhancement is typically heterogeneous. Recognition of these imaging patterns aids in accurate preoperative diagnosis and treatment planning.
